creator: freebsd@grem.de
text:
On Wed, 08 Jan 2014 21:10:24 +0100
Matthias Andree <matthias.andree@gmx.de> wrote:

> Michael,
> 
> could you please look at the PR 185569
> <http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=185569>? It contains a bug
> report about a port you maintain, devel/ice - the port may require
> changes to build with Berkeley DB 6, and it may be diligent to contact
> the upstream maintainers about those changes.
> 
> Upgrade instructions are at
> <http://docs.oracle.com/cd/E17076_03/html/installation/index.html>,
> chapter 9.
> 
> Thank you.
> 

There is little hope for them (ZeroC) to support db6 on my demand -
unless there is a commercial interest. I opened a support request in
their system anyway, asking what their plans are - and then found that
it already had been discussed in detail so definitely not for the 3.5
series - it's basically about licensing issues:
http://www.zeroc.com/forums/bug-reports/6064-ice-3-5-0-wont-compile-berkeley-db-6-0-a.html

I tried reproducing the problem using Ice 3.5.0 as well as Ice 3.5.1
(from http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/184453) on
10-BETA and 9.2-RELEASE, gcc and clang, by explicitly installing
databases/db6 and building/installing devel/ice afterwards, but every
time the port did the right thing and installed databases/db5 as a
dependency and therefore built/installed devel/ice successfully.

Do you (David) maybe use a custom make.conf?

-- 
Michael Gmelin
